engine: SGLang v0.5.13.post1 (TVM_FFI_GPU_BACKEND=cuda, SGLANG_ENABLE_SPEC_V2=0, num_steps=3 topk=4 draft=8)
method: scripts/specforge/perf_devstral_spec.py — pure-decode tok/s via short/long delta (prefill cancels), warmup, ignore_eos, temp 0, 3 coding prompts
target_note: Measured on the text-only Devstral decoder (Ministral3ForCausalLM). The FULL VLM ship (LlavaForConditionalGeneration) cannot attach EAGLE3 in sglang -- the wrapper lacks set_eagle3_layers_to_capture; text-decoder weights are identical so the decode speedup is representative.

results.note: no-spec only; spec pass not completed
INFEASIBLE on 2x24GB: no-spec KV pool was ~82K tokens @ mem 0.82 (126K prompt exceeded it); +spec draft+cuda-graphs shrink the pool further. 128K no-spec needs mem~0.90/fp8 KV.

takeaway: Devstral EAGLE3 draft: gentle decay WITHIN the draft's <=32K regime (2.26x short / 1.91x @16K / 1.79x @30K, accept 3.3->2.7), then a hard memory wall at 128K on 2x24GB (spec pool can't hold it) plus expected deeper accept-collapse. A <=~32-64K win on 32GB cards (R9700's deliverable/lane); for our 256K agentic mission (SWE p90 ~82K) it's marginal -- NGRAM (draft-free, ships on coder-30b ~2.6x @172K) is our depth-surviving spec, not EAGLE3.
